name: Basic Holiday Butter Cookies-pt 2
recipeInstructions:
1. CUT-OUTS: On a floured surface, roll chilled dough to 1/4 inch thick. Flour cookie cutters and cut out shapes. Lift onto prepared baking sheets. Decorate before or after baking. For vibrant food paint, mix 1 egg yolk with 1 tsp water. Divide into 4-6 small bowls or a muffin pan. Add only a small amount of food coloring to each bowl for varied intensities. Use food paintbrushes or cotton swabs to paint cookies; colors deepen during baking. Alternatively, decorate after baking by piping frosting onto cooled cookies. The number of finished cookies depends on cutter size and shape.
2. SPRITZ: If desired, add food coloring to dough. Fill cookie press, gun, or pastry bag with the colored batter. Use appropriate dies or tips as directed. Manufacturer instructions yield about 35 cookies from one dough disk.
3. CHOCOLATE CARAMEL MARBLES: Melt and cool 4 oz chocolate. Knead into 1 dough disk until marbled. Cut 6 caramels into quarters. Form a 3/4 inch ball of chocolate-marble dough around each caramel piece. Place on baking sheets. Chill for 20 minutes, then bake for 15 minutes. Yields 24 cookies.
4. MACAROON WREATHS: Tint one dough disk and 2 cups coconut with green food coloring. Combine the colored dough and coconut; roll into 24 tubes about 5 inches long. Join ends to form wreaths. Brush with egg white, sprinkle remaining coconut, and place 5 red cinnamon candies on each wreath. Bake until lightly golden, approximately 15 minutes. Yields 24 cookies.
